de.title: Private Investmentgelegenheiten Schweiz | Off-Market Zugang | Global Key Partners
de.h1: Private Investmentgelegenheiten & Off-Market-Zugang in der Schweiz
de.meta_description: GKP verbindet Investoren mit privaten Investmentgelegenheiten in der Schweiz. Off-Market-Immobilien, Unternehmensübernahmen und Family-Office-Deal-Flow in Zürich, Zug, Luzern und darüber hinaus.
de.intro: Die Schweiz ist eines der weltweit führenden Ziele für Privatvermögen, Family Offices und institutionelles Kapital. Die einzigartige Kombination aus politischer Stabilität, Finanzinfrastruktur, Rechtssicherheit und einer Kultur der Diskretion macht sie zu einem unvergleichlichen Knotenpunkt für Off-Market-Transaktionen.

body.text: Global Key Partners hat seinen Hauptsitz in Cham, Zug, im Herzen des dynamischsten Unternehmenskantons der Schweiz. Unsere Netzwerke umspannen das gesamte Schweizer Privatmarktgefüge: von Genfs internationalen Bankkreisen über Zürichs Family-Office-Ökosystem bis hin zu Zugs New-Economy-Unternehmern, Basels Pharmakapital und Luzerns etabliertem Privatvermögen.

body.heading: Warum die Schweiz
body.text: Die Schweiz beherbergt das weltweit grösste grenzüberschreitende Vermögensverwaltungszentrum mit über 2,2 Billionen USD in verwalteten Vermögen. Die Kombination aus politischer Neutralität, robuster Rechtsstaatlichkeit, Bankdiskretion und einer Kultur der finanziellen Zurückhaltung ist weltweit einzigartig.

body.heading: Schweizer Märkte, die wir bedienen
bullets: Zürich: Finanzhauptstadt der Schweiz; höchste HNWI-Konzentration; wichtigster Family-Office-Hub
Genf: internationalste Schweizer Stadt; Privatbanken, UN-Ökosystem, Rohstoffhandelskapital
Zug: GKP-Heimatbasis; Unternehmer, New Economy, Crypto Valley, steuereffiziente Domizile
Luzern: etabliertes Zentralschweizer Vermögen; Hospitality, Immobilien, Familienkapital
Basel: Pharma- und Kunsthauptstadt; Novartis/Roche-Ökosystem; Art Basel; BIZ; grenzüberschreitendes DACH

body.heading: Was GKP in der Schweiz tut
bullets: Erschliessung und Vermittlung von Off-Market-Immobilien in allen wichtigen Schweizer Kantonen
Verbindung internationaler Investoren mit Schweizer Family Offices und Privatkapital
Unterstützung bei Unternehmensübernahmen und Nachfolgesituationen im Schweizer KMU-Markt
Strategische Kontaktvermittlung zwischen internationalen Unternehmen und Schweizer Partnern
Unterstützung beim Markteintritt in die Schweiz für qualifizierte ausländische Unternehmen

faqs.q: Warum ist die Schweiz ein führendes Zentrum für Privatkapital und Family Offices?
faqs.a: Die Schweiz vereint politische Neutralität, Rechtsstaatlichkeit, Bankdiskretionstradition, eine starke Währung und eine Kultur der finanziellen Zurückhaltung, die weltweit unübertroffen ist.

faqs.q: Welche Off-Market-Investitionsmöglichkeiten findet GKP in der Schweiz?
faqs.a: GKP erschliesst Off-Market-Immobilien, Unternehmensübernahmen, Hospitality-Assets und strategische Investitionsmöglichkeiten in der ganzen Schweiz. Möglichkeiten, die nicht öffentlich gelistet sind und über vertrauenswürdige Vermittler und private Netzwerke gehandelt werden.

faqs.q: In welchen Schweizer Städten ist GKP tätig?
faqs.a: GKP ist in allen wichtigen Schweizer Vermögenszentren tätig: Zürich, Genf, Zug, Luzern und Basel. Unser Hauptsitz befindet sich in Cham, Zug.

faqs.q: Können Ausländer über GKP Schweizer Immobilien kaufen?
faqs.a: Ja. GKP arbeitet mit qualifizierten ausländischen Investoren zusammen, die Off-Market-Schweizer-Immobilien suchen. Das Lex-Koller-Gesetz schränkt den ausländischen Erwerb von Wohnimmobilien in bestimmten Fällen ein, wir verbinden Kunden mit spezialisierten Rechtsberatern für Strukturierung und Compliance.

en.title: Private Investment Opportunities Switzerland | Off-Market Access | Global Key Partners
en.h1: Private Investment Opportunities & Off-Market Access in Switzerland
en.meta_description: GKP connects investors with private investment opportunities across Switzerland, off-market real estate, business acquisitions, and family office deal flow in Zurich, Zug, Lucerne and beyond.
en.intro: Switzerland is one of the world's premier destinations for private wealth, family offices, and institutional capital. Its combination of political stability, financial infrastructure, legal certainty, and discretionary culture makes it an unrivalled hub for off-market deal activity.

body.text: Global Key Partners is headquartered in Cham, Zug, at the heart of Switzerland's most dynamic entrepreneurial canton. Our networks span the full breadth of the Swiss private market: from Geneva's international banking circles to Zurich's family office ecosystem, from Zug's new-economy entrepreneurs to Basel's pharma wealth and Lucerne's established private capital.

body.heading: Why Switzerland
body.text: Switzerland hosts the world's largest cross-border wealth management centre, with over USD 2.2 trillion in managed assets. Its combination of political neutrality, robust rule of law, banking confidentiality, and a culture of discretion is unmatched globally. For private investors, family offices, and internationally mobile capital, Switzerland remains the premier destination, and off-market access is how the most significant transactions here get done.

body.heading: Swiss markets GKP serves
bullets: Zurich: Switzerland's financial capital; highest HNWI concentration; major family office hub
Geneva: most international Swiss city; private banking, UN ecosystem, commodity trading wealth
Zug: GKP's home base; entrepreneurs, new economy, crypto valley, tax-efficient domiciles
Lucerne: established Central Swiss wealth; hospitality, real estate, family capital
Basel: pharma and art capital; Novartis/Roche ecosystem; Art Basel; BIS; cross-border DACH

body.heading: What GKP does in Switzerland
bullets: Sources and facilitates off-market real estate across all major Swiss cantons
Connects international investors with Swiss family offices and private capital
Facilitates access to business acquisitions and succession situations in the Swiss SME market
Facilitates strategic introductions between international companies and Swiss partners
Supports inbound market entry into Switzerland for qualified foreign companies

faqs.q: Why is Switzerland a leading hub for private capital and family offices?
faqs.a: Switzerland combines political neutrality, robust rule of law, banking confidentiality traditions, a strong currency, and a culture of financial discretion that is globally unmatched. These factors have made it the world's largest cross-border wealth management centre and a preferred domicile for family offices and UHNWI investors from Asia, the Middle East, and Europe.

faqs.q: What off-market investment opportunities does GKP find in Switzerland?
faqs.a: GKP sources off-market real estate, business acquisitions, hospitality assets, and strategic investment opportunities across Switzerland. Our focus is on opportunities not publicly listed, assets and businesses transacted through trusted intermediaries and private networks, before they reach open market channels.

faqs.q: Which Swiss cities does GKP operate in?
faqs.a: GKP operates across all major Swiss wealth centres: Zurich, Geneva, Zug, Lucerne, and Basel. Our home base is Cham, Zug. Each city has a distinct private market profile, we serve all of them.

faqs.q: Can foreign investors buy Swiss real estate through GKP?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP works with qualified foreign investors seeking off-market Swiss real estate. Switzerland's Lex Koller legislation restricts foreign purchase of residential property in certain circumstances, we connect clients with specialist legal counsel to navigate structuring and compliance from the outset.

faqs.q: Does GKP work with Asian and Middle Eastern investors seeking Swiss exposure?
faqs.a: Yes: this is one of GKP's core corridors. We work with Singapore, Hong Kong, Malaysian, Thai, and Gulf-based family offices and UHNWI investors seeking Swiss real estate, business acquisitions, and private capital co-investment. Our founders have direct professional experience across Southeast Asia and Switzerland.

fr.title: Opportunités d'Investissement Privé en Suisse | Accès Hors Marché | Global Key Partners
fr.h1: Opportunités d'investissement privé & accès hors marché en Suisse
fr.meta_description: GKP connecte les investisseurs avec des opportunités d'investissement privé en Suisse, immobilier hors marché, acquisitions d'entreprises et deal flow de family offices à Zurich, Genève, Zoug, Bâle et Lucerne.
fr.intro: La Suisse est l'une des premières destinations mondiales pour les fortunes privées, les family offices et les capitaux institutionnels. Sa combinaison de stabilité politique, d'infrastructure financière, de sécurité juridique et de culture discrétionnaire en fait un hub inégalé pour les transactions hors marché.

body.text: Global Key Partners opère à l'intersection des marchés privés suisses et des flux de capitaux mondiaux. Basé à Cham, Zoug, GKP est intégré dans l'écosystème financier et entrepreneurial suisse, avec des réseaux actifs à Zurich, Genève, Zoug, Bâle et Lucerne.

body.heading: Pourquoi la Suisse
body.text: La Suisse abrite le plus grand centre mondial de gestion de fortune transfrontalière, avec plus de 2 200 milliards USD d'actifs sous gestion. Elle accueille l'une des plus fortes concentrations de family offices, de relations de banque privée et d'investisseurs UHNWI en Europe.

body.heading: Marchés suisses que nous servons
bullets: Zurich: capitale financière suisse ; plus forte concentration HNWI ; hub de family offices majeur
Genève: ville suisse la plus internationale ; banque privée, écosystème ONU, richesses du négoce
Zoug: base de GKP ; entrepreneurs, nouvelle économie, Crypto Valley, domiciles fiscalement avantageux
Lucerne: patrimoine privé du centre suisse ; hôtellerie, immobilier, capital familial
Bâle: capitale pharma et artistique ; écosystème Novartis/Roche ; Art Basel ; BRI ; DACH transfrontalier

faqs.q: Pourquoi la Suisse est-elle un hub majeur pour les capitaux privés et les family offices ?
faqs.a: La Suisse combine neutralité politique, état de droit robuste, traditions de confidentialité bancaire, une monnaie forte et une culture de discrétion financière sans équivalent mondial. Ces facteurs en ont fait le plus grand centre mondial de gestion de fortune transfrontalière.

faqs.q: Les investisseurs étrangers peuvent-ils acheter de l'immobilier suisse via GKP ?
faqs.a: Oui. GKP travaille avec des investisseurs étrangers qualifiés à la recherche d'immobilier suisse hors marché. La législation Lex Koller suisse restreint l'achat étranger de propriétés résidentielles dans certaines circonstances, nous connectons les clients avec des conseils juridiques spécialisés pour la structuration et la conformité dès le départ.

faqs.q: GKP travaille-t-il avec des investisseurs asiatiques et du Moyen-Orient cherchant une exposition suisse ?
faqs.a: Oui: c'est l'un des corridors principaux de GKP. Nous travaillons avec des family offices et investisseurs UHNWI basés à Singapour, Hong Kong, en Malaisie, en Thaïlande et dans le Golfe cherchant de l'immobilier suisse, des acquisitions d'entreprises et des co-investissements.

en.title: Off-Market Real Estate & Investment Opportunities Zurich | Family Office Access | GKP
en.h1: Off-Market Investment Opportunities & Private Capital Access in Zurich
en.meta_description: GKP connects investors and family offices with off-market real estate and private investment opportunities in Zurich, Switzerland's financial capital, accessed through trusted relationships.
en.intro: Zurich is Switzerland's financial capital and one of Europe's most important private wealth centres. Home to major private banks, family offices, institutional investors, and a dense UHNWI population, Zurich offers unparalleled access to private capital, for those with the right relationships.

body.text: GKP is actively present in Zurich's private markets, with relationships across the financial district, the wealth management community, and the entrepreneurial ecosystem that has made the city a hub for technology, private equity, and institutional capital.

body.heading: Zurich's private capital landscape
body.text: Zurich is home to UBS (Switzerland's largest bank), Zurich Insurance, and dozens of private banking institutions managing hundreds of billions in client assets. It hosts the most concentrated HNWI population in Switzerland and a growing number of multi-family offices and independent asset managers serving international clients. The city's real estate market is one of Europe's most competitive, and most opaque, with premium assets routinely transacting off-market through trusted channels.

body.heading: What GKP does in Zurich
bullets: Off-market real estate: luxury apartments, commercial buildings, and development sites
Introductions to Zurich-based family offices and UHNWI capital
Business acquisition mandates in the Zurich SME market
Strategic partnerships for companies seeking Swiss credibility and market access
Capital introduction for founders and investors active in Zurich

body.heading: Zurich for international clients
body.text: Many of GKP's mandates involving Zurich come from international clients, investors from the Gulf, Asia, and Europe seeking access to the Zurich market but lacking the local relationships and presence to operate effectively. GKP acts as their trusted, permanently resident partner in Zurich, providing market intelligence, introductions, and on-the-ground representation.

faqs.q: How do I find off-market real estate in Zurich?
faqs.a: Off-market real estate in Zurich is almost exclusively accessed through trusted intermediary relationships. GKP maintains active connections with property owners, developers, and real estate advisors in Zurich who share opportunities with the firm before they reach public channels. We work on qualified buyer mandates to actively source matching properties.

faqs.q: Are there family offices I can connect with in Zurich?
faqs.a: Zurich has a significant concentration of single and multi-family offices, many of which operate discreetly without public profiles. GKP maintains relationships with Zurich-based family offices who are selectively open to co-investment opportunities, introductions to deal flow, and strategic partnerships.

faqs.q: Can GKP help me access Zurich's private real estate market as an international buyer?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP regularly assists international buyers, from Europe, the Gulf, and Asia, in accessing Zurich's premium off-market real estate. We provide local market expertise, relationship access, and representation throughout the buying process.

en.title: Private Investment Opportunities Zug | Crypto Valley & Off-Market Access | GKP
en.h1: Private Investment Opportunities & Off-Market Access in Zug
en.meta_description: GKP is headquartered in Zug. Switzerland's entrepreneur capital and Crypto Valley. We connect investors with off-market real estate and private investment opportunities in the Zug ecosystem.
en.intro: Zug is home to Global Key Partners. It is also Switzerland's most dynamic entrepreneurial canton, a tax-advantaged, internationally connected hub that attracts founders, holding structures, and new economy wealth from around the world. The combination of traditional Swiss private wealth and digital-native capital makes Zug one of the world's most interesting private capital markets.

body.text: As a Zug-based firm, GKP has deep roots in the local ecosystem. We know the key players, the relevant professional networks, and the private opportunities that arise in one of Switzerland's fastest-growing wealth centres.

body.heading: Zug's unique capital ecosystem
body.text: Zug's low tax rates attract holding companies, trading firms, and crypto businesses from around the world. The Crypto Valley, centred in Zug, has produced a significant cohort of newly wealthy entrepreneurs and investors who are actively allocating to real assets, private businesses, and lifestyle investments. GKP bridges these two worlds: traditional Swiss wealth infrastructure and the new economy capital that Zug increasingly represents.

body.heading: GKP's Zug services
bullets: Off-market real estate access in Zug and the surrounding lake region
Introductions to Zug-based family offices, holding structures, and tech entrepreneurs
Business acquisition mandates for buyers and sellers in the Zug market
Capital introduction to Zug's new economy investor community
Strategic partnerships for companies seeking to establish or grow in Zug

faqs.q: What is special about Zug as a business and investment location?
faqs.a: Zug offers some of Switzerland's lowest corporate and personal tax rates, an efficient and business-friendly administration, and proximity to Zurich's financial infrastructure. It is home to Crypto Valley, a global centre for blockchain and digital asset companies, and attracts a growing concentration of entrepreneurs, holding structures, and international investors.

faqs.q: Why is GKP based in Zug?
faqs.a: GKP is headquartered in Cham, Zug, because its founders have deep roots in the local ecosystem and because Zug represents the intersection of traditional Swiss business values and the new economy capital flows that are defining the next generation of private wealth. It is also an ideal base for reaching both Zurich and the wider central Swiss market.

Es beherbergt das Crypto Valley und zieht eine wachsende Konzentration von Unternehmern und internationalen Investoren an.

en.title: Off-Market Real Estate & Investment Opportunities Lucerne | Central Switzerland | GKP
en.h1: Private Investment Opportunities & Off-Market Access in Lucerne
en.meta_description: GKP connects investors with off-market real estate, hospitality assets, and private investment opportunities in Lucerne and Central Switzerland, one of Europe's most discreet private markets.
en.intro: Lucerne sits at the heart of Switzerland: geographically, culturally, and increasingly, in terms of private wealth. Central Switzerland's combination of natural prestige, discretion, and proximity to Zug's entrepreneurial ecosystem makes Lucerne a quietly significant private market.

body.text: GKP has deep roots in the Lucerne and Central Switzerland ecosystem. Co-founder Dennis Tong's professional background includes years at Gübelin Gem Lab in Lucerne, one of the world's most respected luxury institutions, building relationships across the region's UHNWI and business owner community.

body.heading: Lucerne's private market character
body.text: Unlike Zurich's corporate density or Zug's new-economy concentration, Lucerne's wealth is more traditional: established business families, hospitality and real estate assets built over generations, and a UHNWI community that values discretion above profile. Off-market transactions are the norm rather than the exception here.

body.heading: What GKP does in Lucerne
bullets: Off-market real estate in Lucerne city, the Vierwaldstättersee lakefront, and surrounding alpine areas
Business acquisition introductions in Central Switzerland's established SME market
Connections to Lucerne-based UHNWI families and long-established private capital
Hospitality assets: historic hotels, resort properties, and lifestyle investments in the region
Strategic introductions for international buyers seeking access to Central Switzerland

body.heading: The Zug–Lucerne corridor
body.text: GKP's home base in Cham, Zug places it naturally at the intersection of Zug's entrepreneurial capital and Lucerne's established wealth. This corridor, a 20-minute drive, is one of the most concentrated private wealth zones in Switzerland, and one of the least publicly visible. GKP is embedded in it.

faqs.q: Does GKP have off-market real estate in Lucerne?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP sources off-market residential and hospitality properties in Lucerne, along the Vierwaldstättersee, and in the wider Central Switzerland region. These opportunities are accessed through personal relationships with local owners and advisors, not through public listing platforms.

faqs.q: Why is Central Switzerland an interesting area for private investment?
faqs.a: Central Switzerland: Lucerne, Zug, Schwyz, Nidwalden: combines low tax rates, outstanding quality of life, natural prestige, and a deeply established private wealth community. It is one of the most undersupplied and least publicly marketed investment regions in Switzerland.

faqs.q: Can GKP introduce me to business owners or family offices in Lucerne?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP maintains relationships with business owners, established families, and local professionals in the Lucerne and Central Switzerland area. Introductions are made selectively and only where there is genuine bilateral fit.

de.intro: Luzern liegt im Herzen der Schweiz: geografisch, kulturell und zunehmend auch in Bezug auf Privatvermögen. Die Kombination aus natürlichem Prestige, Diskretion und der Nähe zum unternehmerischen Ökosystem von Zug macht Luzern zu einem stillen, aber bedeutenden Privatmarkt.

body.text: GKP ist tief im Ökosystem Luzerns und der Zentralschweiz verwurzelt. Mitgründer Dennis Tong hat langjährige Erfahrung bei Gübelin Gem Lab in Luzern, einer der renommiertesten Luxusinstitutionen der Welt, und hat dabei Beziehungen zu vermögenden Privatpersonen und Unternehmern der Region aufgebaut.

body.heading: Der Charakter des Luzerner Privatmarkts
body.text: Im Gegensatz zur unternehmerischen Dichte Zürichs oder der New-Economy-Konzentration von Zug ist Luzerns Vermögen traditioneller: etablierte Unternehmerfamilien, Generationen aufgebaute Hospitality- und Immobilienwerte, und vermögende Familien, die Diskretion über öffentliche Sichtbarkeit stellen.

body.heading: Was GKP in Luzern tut
bullets: Off-Market-Immobilien in der Stadt Luzern, am Vierwaldstättersee und in umliegenden Alpenregionen
Vermittlung von Unternehmensübernahmen im etablierten KMU-Markt der Zentralschweiz
Verbindungen zu vermögenden Luzerner Familien und langjährig etabliertem Privatkapital
Hospitality-Assets: historische Hotels, Ferienimmobilien und Lifestyle-Investments in der Region
Strategische Kontaktvermittlung für internationale Käufer mit Interesse an der Zentralschweiz

body.heading: Der Zug-Luzern-Korridor
body.text: GKPs Hauptsitz in Cham, Zug platziert es natürlich an der Schnittstelle zwischen Zugs Unternehmerkapital und Luzerns etabliertem Vermögen. Dieser Korridor. 20 Autominuten: ist eine der konzentriertesten Privatvermögenszonen der Schweiz, und eine der am wenigsten öffentlich sichtbaren.

faqs.q: Hat GKP Off-Market-Immobilien in Luzern?
faqs.a: Ja. GKP erschliesst Off-Market-Wohn- und Hospitality-Immobilien in Luzern, am Vierwaldstättersee und in der weiteren Zentralschweiz, über persönliche Beziehungen zu lokalen Eigentümern und Beratern, nicht über öffentliche Inserate.

faqs.q: Warum ist die Zentralschweiz ein interessanter Privatinvestitionsstandort?
faqs.a: Zentralschweiz: Luzern, Zug, Schwyz, Nidwalden: vereint niedrige Steuersätze, aussergewöhnliche Lebensqualität, natürliches Prestige und eine tief etablierte Privatvermögensgemeinschaft. Es ist eine der am wenigsten öffentlich vermarkteten Investitionsregionen der Schweiz.

faqs.q: Kann GKP mich mit Unternehmenseignern oder Family Offices in Luzern bekannt machen?
faqs.a: Ja. GKP pflegt Beziehungen zu Unternehmenseignern, etablierten Familien und lokalen Fachleuten in Luzern und der Zentralschweiz. Kontakte werden selektiv und nur bei echter beidseitiger Übereinstimmung vorgenommen.

en.title: Off-Market Investment Opportunities Geneva | Private Wealth & Family Office Access | GKP
en.meta_description: GKP connects investors with off-market real estate, business acquisitions, and private capital in Geneva. Switzerland's international wealth capital. Trusted access and introductions.
en.intro: Geneva is Switzerland's most international city and one of Europe's foremost private wealth centres. Home to over 400 financial institutions, dozens of family offices, and a dense concentration of UHNWI residents, it represents an unparalleled market for discreet, off-market transactions.

body.text: GKP is headquartered in Zug but operates actively in Geneva, where its network spans private banking circles, family office principals, international organisations, and the luxury real estate market. Geneva's wealth ecosystem is distinct from Zurich's, more internationally oriented, diplomatically connected, and with a particularly high density of non-Swiss-domiciled wealth.

body.heading: Why Geneva
body.text: Geneva hosts the world's highest concentration of international organisations per capita, a fact that shapes its private wealth landscape: principals and senior executives at global institutions routinely settle here, creating sustained demand for discreet real estate and investment introductions. The city is also home to a significant cluster of commodity trading companies and their principals, a wealth segment with specific off-market investment needs that GKP is positioned to serve.

body.heading: Geneva real estate: what investors should know
body.text: Geneva's residential property market is among Switzerland's most constrained: low vacancy rates, strict construction limitations, and a large international buyer population create persistent supply scarcity. Off-market access is particularly valuable here, many transactions are completed before a property ever reaches a listing platform. GKP facilitates exactly these introductions.

body.heading: What GKP offers in Geneva
bullets: Off-market residential and commercial real estate access in Geneva and the Lake Geneva region
Introductions to Geneva-based family offices and private capital for qualified opportunities
Business acquisition introductions in the Geneva and Romandy market
Strategic introductions for international companies seeking Swiss headquarters or presence
Hospitality and lifestyle asset introductions across the Lake Geneva arc

faqs.q: Does GKP operate in Geneva?
faqs.a: Yes. While GKP is headquartered in Zug, it maintains active relationships in Geneva across private banking, family office, and real estate networks. For Geneva-specific mandates, GKP works directly and through trusted local partners.

faqs.q: How does Geneva's real estate market differ from Zurich's?
faqs.a: Geneva's residential market is constrained by geography (bounded by France and the lake), strict building regulations, and exceptionally high international demand. Vacancy rates are among Switzerland's lowest. Off-market access is particularly valuable in Geneva as a result, many premium properties change hands privately. GKP facilitates access to this off-market layer.

faqs.q: What types of investors are most active in Geneva?
faqs.a: Geneva attracts a particularly international wealth profile: senior executives of international organisations, commodity trading principals, private banking clients from across Europe and the Middle East, and a significant French cross-border community. en.body: Switzerland and Singapore share a natural affinity: both are politically stable, fiscally disciplined, and home to sophisticated private wealth. faqs.q: Can Singapore investors buy Swiss real estate through GKP?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP works with qualified Singapore-based investors, family offices, UHNWIs, and strategic buyers, seeking off-market Swiss real estate. We manage the introduction process and connect clients with specialist legal advisors on Lex Koller compliance, structuring, and cantonal requirements for foreign buyers.

faqs.q: What is Lex Koller and does it affect Singapore buyers?
faqs.a: Lex Koller (the Federal Act on the Acquisition of Real Estate by Persons Abroad) restricts foreign nationals from purchasing Swiss residential property in most circumstances. It does not apply to commercial real estate, which foreign investors may purchase freely. For residential property, specific cantonal quotas and permitted use categories exist. GKP connects clients with specialist legal counsel who navigate these requirements routinely.

faqs.q: Does GKP have an office in Singapore?
faqs.a: GKP is headquartered in Cham, Zug, Switzerland. We do not maintain a permanent Singapore office, our value to Singapore clients lies precisely in GKP's Swiss-side presence and embedded relationships in the Swiss private market. For Singapore-connected mandates, GKP works through trusted local partners and its founders' direct regional networks.

faqs.q: What types of Swiss investments are most relevant for Singapore family offices?
faqs.a: Singapore family offices active in Switzerland typically seek off-market residential and commercial real estate (particularly in Zurich, Zug, Lucerne, and alpine resort markets), hospitality assets, business acquisitions in succession situations, and co-investment alongside Swiss-based operators. GKP sources and introduces opportunities across all these categories.

High-net-worth families and business owners seek stable, rule-of-law jurisdictions for real estate holdings and business acquisitions. faqs.q: Can Malaysian investors access Swiss real estate through GKP?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP works with qualified Malaysian investors seeking off-market Swiss real estate. We manage the introduction process and connect clients with specialist legal advisors on Lex Koller requirements and structuring options for foreign buyers.

faqs.q: Does Lex Koller apply to Malaysian buyers of Swiss property?
faqs.a: Yes. Lex Koller applies to all non-Swiss resident buyers of Swiss residential real estate. Commercial property is not restricted. GKP connects Malaysian clients with specialist Swiss legal counsel experienced in navigating Lex Koller for non-resident buyers, including permitted cantonal quota allocations.

faqs.q: Does GKP have connections in Kuala Lumpur or Malaysia?
faqs.a: GKP is headquartered in Cham, Zug, Switzerland. Our founders have direct professional and personal networks across Southeast Asia, including Malaysia. We work through trusted regional partners for Malaysian-connected mandates.

faqs.a: Yes. GKP supports qualified Malaysian companies seeking to establish relationships, find partners, or connect with investors in Switzerland and Europe. GKP facilitates introductions through its founders' direct Swiss networks and broader European relationships.

en.title: Swiss Investment for German Investors & Family Offices | Off-Market Switzerland Access | GKP
en.h1: Connecting German Private Capital with Off-Market Swiss Investment Opportunities
en.meta_description: GKP helps German investors, family offices, and Mittelstand owners access off-market real estate, business acquisitions, and private capital opportunities in Switzerland. Your trusted Swiss access partner.
en.intro: Germany is Switzerland's largest economic partner and the single biggest source of inbound private capital into the Swiss real estate and private markets. From Munich family offices to Frankfurt private equity principals to Hamburg Mittelstand dynasties, German private wealth has a deep, longstanding affinity with Switzerland. GKP provides the Swiss-side access that turns this affinity into transactions.

body.text: GKP is headquartered in Zug: directly adjacent to the German-speaking Swiss ecosystem and well within the DACH private wealth network. Our founders and advisors operate fluently across Swiss and German business cultures. For German principals approaching the Swiss market, this means a partner who understands both sides without translation loss.

body.heading: Why German capital chooses Switzerland
body.text: Switzerland's appeal to German private wealth is structural and persistent: a stable, strong currency; lower tax environment across multiple cantons; robust legal certainty; and a private real estate market that has consistently outperformed over the long term. Swiss property, particularly in Zurich, Zug, and the alpine resort markets, is a core allocation for many German family offices and UHNWI investors. Business acquisitions in the Swiss SME market are a growing secondary interest, particularly for Mittelstand families seeking European diversification.

body.heading: What GKP offers German clients
bullets: Off-market Swiss real estate access in Zurich, Geneva, Zug, Basel, and Lucerne
Business acquisition introductions: off-market Swiss SME and succession situations
Capital introduction: connecting German capital with Swiss entrepreneurs and asset owners
Swiss market entry support for German companies seeking a Swiss base or partnership
Strategic introductions across Swiss family office and private banking networks

body.heading: The Basel–Stuttgart–Munich corridor
body.text: Northwestern Switzerland, centred on Basel, sits at the heart of the DACH private wealth corridor. Basel is a 45-minute drive from Stuttgart, under two hours from Munich. GKP's activity in Basel, Zurich, and Zug makes it the natural partner for German capital approaching Switzerland across all asset classes, real estate, business acquisitions, and private co-investment.

body.heading: Swiss residency and domicile for German principals
body.text: An increasing number of German UHNWI and family office principals are establishing Swiss residency, attracted by Zug's and Schwyz's cantonal tax frameworks, proximity to Germany, and quality of life. GKP does not provide tax or residency advice, but supports principals through the introduction process to the right specialists and the right local networks on arrival.

faqs.q: Can German investors buy real estate in Switzerland?
faqs.a: Yes. German investors who are Swiss residents may purchase Swiss real estate without restriction. Non-resident German buyers are subject to Lex Koller for residential property, commercial real estate carries no such restriction. GKP connects German clients with specialist Swiss legal counsel on structuring and compliance from the outset.

faqs.q: What is Lex Koller and does it apply to German buyers?
faqs.a: Lex Koller restricts non-Swiss residents from purchasing Swiss residential real estate without a permit. It applies to German buyers domiciled outside Switzerland. Commercial property is not restricted. Swiss residency removes the restriction entirely. GKP works with specialist legal advisors on all structuring options.

faqs.q: What Swiss cantons are most popular with German investors?
faqs.a: Zug and Schwyz attract German UHNWI investors for their tax efficiency and proximity to Germany. Zurich appeals to those seeking urban real estate and financial market access. Basel is popular for its direct proximity to southwest Germany. Alpine resort cantons. Valais and Graubünden: attract German buyers seeking holiday property within cantonal Lex Koller quotas.

faqs.q: Can GKP help a German Mittelstand family with Swiss business acquisitions?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP works with German strategic and financial buyers seeking off-market business acquisition opportunities in Switzerland, particularly succession situations in Swiss SMEs across manufacturing, professional services, and specialist industries. We source and introduce opportunities that do not reach public M&A platforms.

faqs.q: Does GKP assist German companies seeking to establish in Switzerland?
faqs.a: Yes. GKP supports qualified German companies seeking a Swiss domicile, distribution partner, or strategic introduction into the Swiss market. We facilitate introductions to the right local partners, advisors, and networks in Zug, Zurich, and Basel.

faqs.q: Was ist Lex Koller und gilt es für deutsche Käufer?
faqs.a: Lex Koller schränkt nicht in der Schweiz ansässige Personen beim Kauf von Schweizer Wohnimmobilien ohne Bewilligung ein. Es gilt für ausserhalb der Schweiz domizilierte deutsche Käufer. Gewerbeimmobilien sind nicht eingeschränkt. Schweizer Wohnsitz hebt die Einschränkung vollständig auf.

faqs.q: Can UK and non-dom investors based in London buy Swiss real estate?
faqs.a: Yes. Non-Swiss-resident buyers, including UK nationals and London-based non-doms, can purchase Swiss commercial real estate freely. As UK non-dom policy evolves, Switzerland is increasingly discussed as both an investment destination and an alternative or complementary domicile.

GKP works with these structures on Swiss investment mandates regardless of the principal's origin, provided all applicable compliance requirements are met.

We do not maintain a London office: our value to London-based clients is precisely GKP's Swiss-side presence and embedded relationships with off-market Swiss asset owners.
